questions.title: Diabetes Management
questions.text: A client with type 2 diabetes has a blood glucose level of 45 mg/dL and is confused. Which intervention should the nurse implement first?

choices.text: Give orange juice with added sugar

questions.rationale: For conscious clients with hypoglycemia, oral glucose is the first intervention. Orange juice with sugar provides fast-acting carbohydrates to quickly raise blood glucose levels.

questions.title: Postpartum Assessment
questions.text: A nurse is assessing a client 4 hours after vaginal delivery. Which findings should the nurse report to the healthcare provider? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Urinary output of 100 mL in 4 hours

questions.rationale: Decreased urinary output, elevated temperature, and elevated blood pressure require immediate provider notification as they may indicate complications like infection or preeclampsia. Lochia rubra with small clots and a firm, midline fundus are normal findings in the immediate postpartum period.

questions.title: Medication Calculation
questions.text: The healthcare provider orders Heparin 1,000 units/hour IV. The label reads 25,000 units/500 mL. Calculate the mL/hour to be administered.
questions.rationale: Using dimensional analysis: (1,000 units/hour × 500 mL) ÷ 25,000 units = 20 mL/hour

questions.title: Respiratory Assessment
questions.text: A client has crackles in the lung bases. Which position should the nurse place the client in to facilitate breathing?
questions.rationale: High Fowler's position (sitting upright at a 60-90 degree angle) allows for maximum lung expansion, especially in the bases, which helps mobilize secretions and improve ventilation in clients with crackles.

questions.title: Pain Management
questions.text: A postoperative client reports pain of 7/10 despite receiving the prescribed opioid analgesic 2 hours ago. Which nursing interventions are appropriate? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Administer the prescribed PRN opioid immediately
Assess the client's pain using a comprehensive pain assessment tool
Encourage the client to wait until the next scheduled dose

questions.rationale: Appropriate interventions include notifying the provider about inadequate pain control, performing a comprehensive pain assessment, and offering non-pharmacological measures. The nurse should not administer another dose if it's too early per orders, and should not encourage the client to simply endure uncontrolled pain.
